key_themes: Record 2025 earnings beats and capital returns (buybacks + dividend hike)
Strong Q1 2026 operational activity with 286 transactions, new leases, and financing
Cautious 2026 EPS guidance below consensus amid normalization of asset sales gains
sources_checked: Recent web news on earnings and deals
X keyword and semantic search for $AER sentiment
summary: AerCap delivered exceptional 2025 results (record $3.8B net income, adjusted EPS $15.37, strong beats) with aggressive shareholder returns, robust Q1 2026 deal flow (286 assets transacted, Ethiopian freighter leases, Airbus orders), and continued analyst Buy/Moderate Buy ratings with average price targets of ~$153–$162 above the current ~$147 share price. Stock has returned over 52% in the past year and recovered from the post-earnings dip. Biggest risk is the reset 2026 outlook ($12–13 adjusted EPS, excluding sales gains) plus potential lease-rate pressure, higher financing costs, and geopolitical/insurance issues with airlines like Emirates/Qatar.[[1]](https://www.aercap.com/news-media/press-releases/detail/624/aercap-holdings-n-v-reports-record-financial-results-for)[[2]](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/aercap-shares-fall-3-as-2026-outlook-disappoints-despite-record-2025-results-93CH-4490247)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AER/)

recent_posts.highlight: Record 2025 net income $3.8B ($21.30/share), Q4 beat, full-year guidance issued (later seen as cautious)
AerCap and peers issue fleet recovery notices to Emirates and Qatar over lapsed war risk insurance
286 transactions and ~$3B financing in Q1 2026; new Boeing 777 freighter leases with Ethiopian Airlines
L1 Capital trimmed AER after strong rally — profit-taking, not a broken thesis
Framework post addressing misconceptions around AER and its endgame
